title: Appunia morindoides
taxonomy: Appunia morindoides is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Malpighiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Barbados cherry or acacia family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. The genus Appunia itself is a genus of flowering plants that consists of several species, with Appunia morindoides being one of them. The classification of Appunia morindoides is further detailed as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Malpighiales, family Malpighiaceae, genus Appunia, species A. morindoides.
